labels.notes: Linda + James (Jim) 10:18am — multi-patient session (couple seen together), recorded on a different machine and pulled into Room 6 history via cross-machine sync. User opened it in History and clicked 'Generate Multi-Patient SOAP' at 12:12 EDT → multi-patient SOAP saved with 2 sub-patients (Speaker 1 Jim: medication review for mood/pain/heart, iron infusion follow-up, cancer surveillance; Speaker 2 Linda: separate content). DEFECTS: (1) **Class C — REGRESSION ROOT CAUSE FOUND**: HistoryWindow.tsx line 891-933 ("Default path: full session SOAP regeneration (auto-detects patients)") calls `generateSoapNote` (which returns MultiPatientSoapResult), then serializes ALL notes into one combined string via `serializeSoapNotes(result.notes)` and writes them as a SINGLE concatenated `soap_note.txt` via `save_local_soap_note`. It does NOT call `local_archive::save_multi_patient_soap` to write per-patient files + `patient_labels.json`. The fan-out logic at HistoryWindow:566-586 reads `session.patient_labels` to produce one row per sub-patient — but `patient_labels` stays empty/null because the regen path never writes `patient_labels.json`. CONTRAST: the continuous-mode path (`encounter_pipeline::generate_and_archive_soap`) DOES call `save_multi_patient_soap` when notes.len() > 1, so continuous-mode-detected multi-patient sessions DO fan out correctly in History — only history-regen multi-patient sessions are broken. FIX: in HistoryWindow.tsx, when `result.notes.length > 1` after history regen, call a new IPC (or extend `save_local_soap_note`) that mirrors `save_multi_patient_soap` semantics — write `soap_patient_N.txt` per note, write `patient_labels.json`, set `metadata.patient_count = N`. Also extend `save_multi_patient_soap` to write a stub `metadata.json` when the session dir only has SOAP and no metadata (server-only orphan case). (2) **Class C/G — orphan partial-archive**: local archive dir has ONLY soap_note.txt, no metadata.json and no transcript.txt. Local `save_multi_patient_soap` silently no-ops the metadata update because metadata_path doesn't exist (line 970: `if metadata_path.exists()`). Either fail loudly when called against a server-only session, or write a stub metadata.json from server-fetched details before the SOAP write. Replay tooling will skip this session entirely (no replay_bundle.json). Per GT both sub-patients' SOAPs are clinically correct; the failure is metadata + history-list rendering.
